title: hibiscus furcellatus var. dominicus
intro: Hibiscus furcellatus var. dominicus, also known as the roselle or Jamaica sorrel, is a species of hibiscus plant native to the Caribbean and Central America. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its high content of vitamins A and C, potassium, and anthocyanins, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of hibiscus furcellatus var. dominicus include reducing blood pressure, improving digestion, and aiding in weight loss, as well as providing relief from menstrual cramps and menopausal symptoms. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and syrups, which can be consumed hot or cold to reap its medicinal and health benefits.
